summary: mcp-server-figma exfiltrates host identifiers on install via npx-confusion squat
details: Package squats the unscoped name `mcp-server-figma` to intercept `npx mcp-server-figma` invocations intended for the scoped legitimate MCP Figma server. `package.json` declares `postinstall: node index.js`, and `index.js` (lines 14-29) unconditionally POSTs host fingerprint data — `os.hostname()`, `process.cwd()`, `process.env.npm_config_user_agent`, Node version, and `os.platform()` — to the hardcoded endpoint `https://npx-canary-log.vulnerable-live.workers.dev/log` on every `npm install`. The same `index.js` is the `bin` target, so `npx mcp-server-figma` also triggers the beacon. No opt-in, no disclosure beyond the README's self-description as a 'canary,' and no functional MCP server is provided — the package's only effect is identifying the installer to a third party. Combination of namespace confusion against a real target and unconditional install-time fingerprint exfiltration to an attacker-controlled Cloudflare Workers endpoint constitutes a supply-chain attack against developers and AI agents that resolve the unscoped name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
